title: Mascara on Cotton
stainChemistry: Mascara is waxes (beeswax, carnauba) + oils (mineral oil, lanolin) + iron-oxide pigment (usually black, sometimes brown) + film-forming polymers + preservatives. Waterproof mascara adds silicone and hydrophobic polymers that resist water entirely. On cotton, mascara transfers from eyelashes onto pillowcases, collars, or anywhere near the face. Regular mascara responds to the standard wax+oil pattern (rubbing alcohol → Dawn); waterproof mascara needs a silicone-targeting solvent like micellar water or POG.
whyThisWorks: Catch cloth UNDER + solvent tamp from the TOP pattern. IPA or micellar water breaks the wax + oil + silicone matrix. Dawn emulsifies residual oil. Iron-oxide pigment usually comes out with the wax, rarely needs peroxide.
defaultAssumption: Morning-routine or pillowcase transfer. Most mascara is NOT waterproof and responds to standard IPA + Dawn. Ask about formula if user knows.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cotton towel catch cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Ask: waterproof mascara or regular? Regular = alcohol path. Waterproof = micellar water / POG path. Place a catch cloth UNDER the stain.

spottingProtocol.equipment: IPA 90%+ OR micellar water OR POG on clean white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply solvent to a white cloth. Tamp from the BACK of the fabric over the catch cloth. Flip cloth frequently. 2–3 passes for fresh mascara.
Work Dawn into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Rinse cool.
Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

steps: 1. Waterproof vs regular. Catch cloth UNDER.
2. IPA (regular) or micellar / POG (waterproof) tamp from back.
3. Dawn / NSD to emulsify wax + oil.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Waterproof needs silicone-specific solvent — IPA alone may plateau
Iron-oxide pigment usually comes with the wax, no separate pigment step

homeSolutions.instruction: Regular mascara comes off with rubbing alcohol; waterproof needs MICELLAR WATER (the makeup-remover bottle at any drugstore) or Goo Gone. The difference matters — alcohol on waterproof mascara barely does anything.

homeSolutions.agent: Put a White Towel UNDER the Stain
homeSolutions.instruction: Fold a clean white towel and place it UNDER the stained area. The dissolved mascara transfers DOWN into the catch cloth, not sideways.

homeSolutions.agent: Solvent on a White Cloth — Tamp From the Back
homeSolutions.instruction: REGULAR mascara: rubbing alcohol on a white cloth. WATERPROOF: micellar water on a cotton round or white cloth. Press onto the stain from the BACK of the fabric. Flip cloth frequently. 2–3 rounds usually clears fresh mascara.
Work a drop of Dawn into the spot with a soft toothbrush to lift any residual wax + oil. Rinse cool.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer. Repeat solvent + Dawn if any color remains.

steps: 1. Waterproof? Use micellar water, not alcohol.
3. Rubbing alcohol (regular) or micellar water (waterproof) on a white cloth, tamp from the back.
5. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if waterproof mascara has been hot-dried and two rounds don't clear it.
warnings: Waterproof needs micellar water, not alcohol
Never pour solvent directly on cotton

neverDo: Never treat waterproof mascara with alcohol alone — plateau
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates IPA, micellar water, POG, NSD, hot wash
Dyed cotton: alcohol usually safe; inside-seam-test if concerned

materialWarnings: Waterproof mascara = micellar water or POG, not alcohol

escalation.when: Waterproof mascara heat-set by dryer, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mascara on cotton — [regular / waterproof], age [fresh / hot-dried], home-treated with [rubbing alcohol / micellar water] + Dawn."

contextQuestions: Has it been through a hot dryer?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Waterproof vs regular is the whole unlock. Waterproof formulas resist alcohol the way they resist tears — need silicone-specific solvent.
